summary: Surah Al-Insan recalls that man was once nothing, then was created from a mixed fluid to be tested and guided to either gratitude or ingratitude. It contrasts the fate of disbelievers (chains, shackles, blazing fire) with the virtues of the righteous who fulfill vows, fear the Day, and feed the needy, orphans, and prisoners purely for Allah’s sake. Their reward is protection from that Day’s evil, joy, silk garments, shade, low-hanging fruits, silver vessels, drinks mixed with camphor and ginger from Salsabil, youthful attendants, and pure drink, with the assurance that their efforts are appreciated. The Qur’an was sent gradually; the Prophet is urged to be patient, remember Allah morning and evening, and worship by night. Many love the fleeting world, but all will is under Allah’s Will; He admits whom He wills to His mercy.
